text.title: Competitive Intelligence Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Competitive Intelligence methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Monitor each signal class at its natural change rate: pricing/funding weekly; feature/website daily; hiring/SEO monthly.
rule.rationale: Polling too slowly misses time-sensitive funding signals; too fast wastes quota on noise.

rule.statement: Every battlecard MUST carry a generation timestamp and a 14-day TTL; older battlecards must not be distributed.
rule.rationale: Sales reps trust dated artifacts; stale-but-confident claims actively damage deals.

rule.statement: Haiku to mechanical collection/classification; Sonnet to synthesis/battlecards; Opus to threat assessment. Never invert.
rule.rationale: Mixing reasoning-grade models into collection adds 10x cost; mixing fast models into threat analysis produces hallucinated claims.

rule.statement: Every digest and battlecard MUST pass a fact-checker that HEAD-requests every cited URL before distribution.
rule.rationale: LLMs invent URLs like /pricing/enterprise that never existed; publishing an invented citation destroys trust.

rule.statement: Every factual claim MUST reference an event_id from the collector NDJSON.
rule.rationale: Unsourced claims are liabilities; provenance is the audit trail.

rule.statement: Collector MUST emit one event per delta; never aggregate inside the collector.
rule.rationale: Aggregated events hide sequence; acceleration patterns vanish.

rule.statement: Prefer competitor APIs, then RSS, then scraping, then screenshot diff - in that order, per competitor.
rule.rationale: APIs are reliable and ToS-compliant; scraping is fragile and legally ambiguous.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
